llm.title: LLM / Models
llm.introBefore: OpenAI-compatible profiles (API key on the server).
llm.introAfter: can select a profile for Analyze / Consolidate / Replay.
llm.storageHint: Put API keys in backend/.env and reference them with Env alias, or paste a key here if your server allows storing it in the database.
llm.newProfileHint: Use Env alias with the same variable name as in backend/.env (restart the backend after changing .env). Leave API key empty for that approach.
llm.labelApiKey: API key (optional, one-time — not returned by the API)
llm.probeFullHint: Runs chat, JSON-style reply, streaming sample, and embeddings (uses EMBED_MODEL from server env). Several API calls.
llm.probeEmbedFromEnv: from server env, same host/key as profile
llm.probeFullUnexpected: Full diagnostic returned an unexpected response. Restart the backend, hard-refresh the admin page, and try again. If it persists, check the browser network tab for the POST /probe response body.
llm.probeLegacyServer: The server on this port is an old build (tiny JSON only). On Windows, several Python processes can all listen on port 8000 — stop them all, then start one backend from backend/: python -m uvicorn app.main:app --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8000 (no reload), or use another free port and set NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL.

ragEval.pageSubtitle: Run batch chat evaluations with isolated history (same as backend test_rag.py). Jobs run in the background; results appear below while processing.
ragEval.chooseSuiteFile: Choose a JSON or CSV file for this suite type.
ragEval.compareHint: Optional: compare JSON (previous report with results array)

idLegend.body: in the previews below are training record IDs (review in the database). For active rules (section 3) they are prompt override IDs. Suggestion 1, 2… is only ordering from the current analysis — they are not database IDs.

supporting.noPreview: No preview of linked training records. Run analysis again (section 2) or refresh the rules list (section 3).
supporting.extraBeyond: +{count} more training records beyond the preview limit.
supporting.trainingRecordMissing: Training record · ID {id} — no preview in database

questionBank.title: 4) Question bank (use in rules)
questionBank.description: Training records linked to analysis, overrides, or the active prompt. Helps avoid duplicate rules for the same questions.
questionBank.empty: No entries (or filters narrow to zero).

systemPromptHead.title: Base system prompt (RAG instructions)
systemPromptHead.intro: This is the first block of the model system message (instructions and JSON schema). Retrieved documents and “Additional rules” from applied overrides are added separately. Edit here to replace the built-in template for all new chats.
systemPromptHead.rulesHint: For incremental bullet rules without replacing the whole prompt, use prompt overrides from your training-quality workflow or database tools (not shown in this screen).
systemPromptHead.viewOnlyHint: Read-only. Click Edit to change the prompt — this avoids accidental edits.
systemPromptHead.saved: Saved. New conversations use the updated base block.
systemPromptHead.clearConfirm: Remove the custom base prompt and restore the built-in template from code?

status.discardPrompt: Optional: why are you discarding this suggestion? (helps the analyzer skip equivalent ideas next time)
status.suggestionDiscarded: Suggestion discarded. Analyzer will avoid repeating it.
status.confirmRollback: Rollback override #{id}? It will stop applying to new conversations.
status.mergeDuplicateHint: Merged text may duplicate the base system prompt. You can edit the base prompt or run merge with Force.
status.noNewSuggestions: No new suggestions for current reviews.
status.noAnalysisYet: No analysis yet. Press Analyze to generate one.
status.noAnalysisClick: No analysis yet. Click Analyze reviews.

applyError.covered: This rule looks already covered by {source} (similarity {score}).
applyError.coveredFallback: This rule looks already covered by the system prompt or an active rule.

reviewSummary.title: 1) What reviewers reported
reviewSummary.description: The analyzer uses exactly these signals: notes, reasoning, and ideal_output from review.
reviewSummary.productionIntro: Set of active overrides + base template. Config fingerprint:
reviewSummary.noActiveRules: No active rules (base system prompt only).

suggestions.title: 2) Suggested rules
suggestions.blurb: One LLM call, 24h cache, limit 3 / 5 min. Each suggestion has 3 actions: Apply, Apply & Test (replay), Discard.
suggestions.duplicateHidden: {count} suggestions hidden because they already exist in the system prompt or active rules.
suggestions.discardedHidden: {count} suggestions hidden because they match a suggestion you previously discarded.
suggestions.questionClaimHidden: {count} suggestions hidden: all linked training records are already claimed by the active prompt or earlier overrides (question bank).

activeOverrides.title: 3) Active rules
activeOverrides.limitHint: Active rules limit: {active}/{max}. You can merge all into one rule (one LLM call, limit 2/min).
activeOverrides.none: No active overrides. Apply a suggestion above first.
activeOverrides.noTestYet: No test yet. Click Test now to replay this rule on its affected examples plus paraphrases.

dashboard.chartNoTickets30d: No tickets in the last 30 days.

adminSettings.title: Admin console
adminSettings.noKnowledgeGaps: No knowledge gaps found for current filter.
adminSettings.ragTopKSaved: Retrieval K saved (SQLite meta; applies immediately).
adminSettings.ingestPreChunkFootnote: These come from server config and ingest code; changing them requires .env and/or code changes except chunk size/overlap (per reindex) and retrieval K (Save above).
adminSettings.ingestChunkHelp: Chunk size and overlap apply per reindex/upload. Retrieval top-K is how many reranked passages are sent to the model on each chat turn (save writes SQLite meta; “Use .env default” removes that override). Ingest embeddings use GET /api/admin/llm/chat-target → ingest_embed_sync.
